method_atom: a cavity-method / message-passing technique from the statistical mechanics of disordered systems that reformulates a single global connectivity constraint of a weighted combinatorial optimization problem into an equivalent set of purely local constraints, yielding both a new optimization algorithm and a way to compute typical-case (ensemble-averaged) properties of the optimal solution over random instances
object_atom: the Minimum Weight Steiner Tree problem — finding the least-total-weight connected subnetwork joining a prescribed set of terminal nodes — studied on random graphs of various types
brief_reason: the local-constraint reformulation plus cavity/message-passing analysis of optimization over random ensembles is the transferable machinery, while the minimum-weight Steiner tree on graphs is the domain-specific object it acts on.
